does: This paper documents the operational status and safety improvements of DALL·E 2 during its research preview phase, following the creation of over 3 million user-generated images. The authors report refinements to their safety processes based on empirical data collected from early user interactions, demonstrating how real-world deployment reveals failure modes and enables iterative safety enhancements. The update reflects lessons learned about content filtering, harmful use prevention, and responsible scaling, with plans to expand access to approximately 1,000 additional users weekly from the existing waitlist.
connects: This work builds on prior research in responsible AI deployment and safety engineering for large generative models, extending the safety frameworks initially outlined in DALL·E 2's technical introduction. It responds to longstanding concerns in the AI safety and ethics literature about the risks of image generation systems including bias, misuse, and unintended harms. The paper connects to broader efforts in the computer vision and ML communities to systematize safety testing through real-world deployment, drawing on methodologies established in earlier studies of text and image model safety at scale.
enables: This work enables future research into how safety processes improve through scaled human feedback and diverse user interactions, establishing a pathway for incrementally expanding access to powerful generative models while maintaining safety guardrails. The documented safety improvements inform approaches to responsible release of similarly capable systems and provide empirical data for developing better safety metrics and detection systems. The staged rollout strategy and safety update processes described here can guide deployment practices for other large-scale generative models and help researchers understand how to balance capability advancement with risk mitigation in public-facing AI systems.
